chapter_title.ar: باب الْكَسِيرِ وَ الْمَجْدُورِ وَ مَنْ بِهِ الْجِرَاحَاتُ وَ تُصِيبُهُمُ الْجَنَابَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 45 – The one with a broken (limb), and the one with pockmarks, and the one with the injuries, and they are hit by the sexual impurity

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/186)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/186)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ بِهِ الْقَرْحُ وَ الْجِرَاحَةُ يُجْنِبُ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِأَنْ لَا يَغْتَسِلَ وَ يَتَيَمَّمَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazzaz, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about the man who happens to be with the sores and the injuries, experiences the sexual impurity. Heasws said: ‘There is no problem with it if he does not wash, and he performs Tayammum’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Mahbub from abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man on whose body there are dimples or wounds and experiences sexual relation. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful if one does not take Ghusl (bath) in such condition, instead takes Tayammum.’’’
